problem_statement:
[dynamo] _io.text_encoding causes a graph break (no polyfill)

### 🐛 Describe the bug

`_io.text_encoding` is a C builtin with no traceable Python implementation, so any call through it graph-breaks under `torch.compile`:

```python
import torch

@torch.compile(backend="eager", fullgraph=True)
def fn(encoding):
    import _io
    return _io.text_encoding(encoding)

fn(None)
```

```
torch._dynamo.exc.Unsupported: Attempted to call function marked as skipped
  Explanation: Dynamo does not know how to trace the builtin `_io.text_encoding.` This function is either a Python builtin (e.g. _warnings.warn) or a third-party C/C++ Python extension (perhaps created with pybind).
  ...
  Developer debug context: module: _io, qualname: text_encoding, skip reason: cannot determine source file for _io (likely a C extension or builtin)
```

This function is called internally by several stdlib APIs that accept an `encoding=None` parameter, e.g. `tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile`, `pathlib.Path.read_text`/`write_text`. It's also what causes `test/cpython/v3_13/test_bool.py::BoolTest.test_fileclosed` to silently fall back to eager (rather than tracing fully) when run with `PYTORCH_TEST_WITH_DYNAMO=1` — that test happens to be decorated with `@torch._dynamo.error_on_graph_break(False)`, so it still reports "ok", just with a `UserWarning` on every run instead of full coverage.

`_io.text_encoding` has a well-known pure-Python equivalent shipped in CPython's own `Lib/_pyio.py`:

```python
def text_encoding(encoding, stacklevel=2):
    if encoding is None:
        if sys.flags.utf8_mode:
            encoding = "utf-8"
        else:
            encoding = "locale"
        if sys.flags.warn_default_encoding:
            import warnings
            warnings.warn("'encoding' argument not specified.",
                          EncodingWarning, stacklevel + 1)
    return encoding
```

This looks like a good candidate for a `torch/_dynamo/polyfills/` entry (`substitute_in_graph`), following the existing pattern for `os.fspath` / `sys.intern`.

### Versions

PyTorch version: 2.14.0a0+git377da73
Is debug build: False
CUDA used to build PyTorch: None
ROCM used to build PyTorch: N/A

OS: macOS 26.5 (arm64)
Clang version: 21.0.0 (clang-2100.0.123.102)
CMake version: version 4.3.2

Python version: 3.13.13 (main, Apr 14 2026, 14:32:41) [Clang 22.1.3] (64-bit runtime)
Python platform: macOS-26.5-arm64-arm-64bit-Mach-O
Is CUDA available: False

CPU:
Apple M5


cc @chauhang @penguinwu @voznesenskym @EikanWang @jgong5 @Guobing-Chen @XiaobingSuper @zhuhaozhe @blzheng @wenzhe-nrv @jiayisunx @kadeng @amjames @jataylo @azahed98
patch:
diff --git a/torch/_dynamo/polyfills/__init__.py b/torch/_dynamo/polyfills/__init__.py
index 1b332c04758ec..d4a07c009d4cc 100644
--- a/torch/_dynamo/polyfills/__init__.py
+++ b/torch/_dynamo/polyfills/__init__.py
@@ -32,6 +32,7 @@
         _collections as _collections,
         builtins as builtins,
         functools as functools,
+        io as io,
         itertools as itertools,
         operator as operator,
         os as os,
diff --git a/torch/_dynamo/polyfills/io.py b/torch/_dynamo/polyfills/io.py
new file mode 100644
index 0000000000000..a75e062f46e46
--- /dev/null
+++ b/torch/_dynamo/polyfills/io.py
@@ -0,0 +1,30 @@
+"""
+Python polyfills for _io
+"""
+
+from __future__ import annotations
+
+import _io
+
+import sys
+import warnings
+
+from ..decorators import substitute_in_graph
+
+
+__all__ = ["text_encoding"]
+
+
+# Copied from Lib/_pyio.py in the standard library
+# pyrefly: ignore [bad-argument-type]
+@substitute_in_graph(_io.text_encoding, can_constant_fold_through=True)
+def text_encoding(encoding: str | None, stacklevel: int = 2, /) -> str:
+    if encoding is None:
+        encoding = "utf-8" if sys.flags.utf8_mode else "locale"
+        if sys.flags.warn_default_encoding:
+            warnings.warn(
+                "'encoding' argument not specified.",
+                EncodingWarning,
+                stacklevel + 1,
+            )
+    return encoding
diff --git a/torch/_dynamo/polyfills/loader.py b/torch/_dynamo/polyfills/loader.py
index cfce3edfb0ab0..9defe564e45fb 100644
--- a/torch/_dynamo/polyfills/loader.py
+++ b/torch/_dynamo/polyfills/loader.py
@@ -19,6 +19,7 @@
     "builtins",
     "copy",
     "functools",
+    "io",
     "itertools",
     "operator",
     "os",
test_patch:
diff --git a/test/dynamo/test_repros.py b/test/dynamo/test_repros.py
index 59c768a156602..f38b27952a77a 100644
--- a/test/dynamo/test_repros.py
+++ b/test/dynamo/test_repros.py
@@ -6506,6 +6506,21 @@ def fn(x):
 
         fn(torch.randn(4))
 
+    # https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/189925
+    def test_io_text_encoding(self):
+        import _io
+
+        @torch.compile(backend="eager", fullgraph=True)
+        def fn(x):
+            enc_explicit = _io.text_encoding("utf-8")
+            enc_default = _io.text_encoding(None)
+            return torch.sin(x), enc_explicit, enc_default
+
+        x = torch.randn(4)
+        _, enc_explicit, enc_default = fn(x)
+        self.assertEqual(enc_explicit, _io.text_encoding("utf-8"))
+        self.assertEqual(enc_default, _io.text_encoding(None))
+
     # https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/88813
     def test_return_value_duplication_tensor(self) -> None:
         def fn(val: torch.Tensor) -> tuple[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor]:
